ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry specific to 7 Up Lemon; closest parent 'carbonated beverage' (FOODON_03301589) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN000 'Soft drinks' is the closest parent category for a lemon-flavoured carbonated soft drink; no product-specific code exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 'Carbonated soft drink' is the closest parent; no product-specific code for 7 Up Lemon exists.

main_ingredients: high fructose corn syrup or sugar

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard single-serve can of 7 Up Lemon is 330 ml, equivalent to approximately 330 g, providing about 139 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: 7 UP Lemon Carbonated Soft Drink — USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values derived from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entries for 7 UP lemon-flavoured carbonated soft drink (per 100 ml / 100 g basis). Values are consistent with standard 7 Up Lemon product formulation. Protein and fat are confirmed zero by product formulation; energy is derived entirely from sugar (carbohydrate).

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of carbonated soft drinks including lemon-lime sodas — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for 7 Up Lemon specifically. GI of 51 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for regular lemon-lime carbonated soft drink (Sprite/7 Up type), which reports GI values in the range 49–53 for sugar-sweetened lemon-lime sodas. A midpoint value of 51 is used. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (51 × 35.0) / 100 = 17.9, rounded to 19.8 using 38.8 g carbohydrate in 330 g serving (10.6 g/100 g × 3.3 = 34.98 g ≈ 35.0 g; GL = 51 × 35.0 / 100 = 17.85 ≈ 17.9). Note: gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (12 mg per 100 ml)
hypertension.guidance: 7 Up Lemon contains only 12 mg sodium per 100 ml, making it a low-sodium beverage; however, the high sugar content may contribute to weight gain and indirectly elevate blood pressure with regular consumption, so moderation is advised for individuals with hypertension.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free sugar content (10.6 g per 100 ml, entirely as added sugar)
type2_diabetes.guidance: 7 Up Lemon provides 10.6 g of free sugars per 100 ml with a GI of approximately 51 and a glycemic load of approximately 17.9 per 330 ml can, making it unsuitable for regular consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; it should be avoided or replaced with a sugar-free variant.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free sugar intake linked to elevated triglycerides and cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages such as 7 Up Lemon is associated with elevated serum triglycerides and increased cardiovascular disease risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake and prefer water or unsweetened beverages.

llm_training.natural_language_description: 7 Up Lemon is a commercially manufactured, lemon-flavoured carbonated soft drink produced by the 7 Up brand (PepsiCo). It is an ultra-processed beverage consisting primarily of carbonated water, sugar (or high-fructose corn syrup), citric acid, and natural lemon and lime flavourings, with potassium citrate added as an acidity regulator and potassium benzoate as a preservative. The drink is clear, effervescent, and characterised by a crisp, sweet-tart lemon-lime flavour profile with a refreshing carbonated mouthfeel. It contains no protein, no fat, and no dietary fibre; its entire caloric content of approximately 42 kcal per 100 ml is derived from free sugars (10.6 g per 100 ml). Sodium content is low at approximately 12 mg per 100 ml. The glycemic index is estimated at 51 (low GI by the ≤55 threshold), but the glycemic load per standard 330 ml can is approximately 17.9, which is clinically significant for individuals managing blood glucose. For people with Type 2 Diabetes, this beverage is high concern due to its free sugar content and should be avoided or replaced with a sugar-free variant. For cardiovascular health, regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with elevated triglycerides. In Singapore, 7 Up Lemon is widely available as a bottled or canned beverage and is commonly served as a cold drink accompaniment at noodle restaurants and hawker stalls. It is sold at The Noodle Place (Circular Road) for SGD 1.80.
common_user_queries: How many calories in 7 Up Lemon?
Is 7 Up Lemon suitable for diabetics?
What is the sugar content of 7 Up Lemon?
What is the GI of 7 Up?
How much sodium is in 7 Up Lemon?
Can I drink 7 Up Lemon if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entries for 7 Up lemon-lime carbonated soft drink, which closely matches the product formulation. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for lemon-lime sodas. No Singapore-specific HPB Foodo entry exists for this branded product. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the analogous GI estimate and the use of USDA Branded Foods rather than a primary regional source.
notes: protein_g is confirmed zero — contains no protein by product formulation (USDA FoodData Central). fat_g is confirmed zero — contains no fat by product formulation (USDA FoodData Central). saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are confirmed zero by product formulation. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero — carbonated soft drink contains no dietary fibre. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source entry for this product. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source (despite lemon flavouring, no ascorbic acid is added at reportable levels). vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. glycemic_index set to 51 (analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for lemon-lime carbonated soft drink); gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule. GL per 330 ml serving = 51 × 34.98 / 100 ≈ 17.8, reported as 19.8 after rounding to one decimal. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the food is served at a Singapore restaurant. The food_group has been corrected from 'Mixed' (input) to 'Beverages' as this is a carbonated soft drink. Price SGD 1.80 preserved from input (The Noodle Place, Circular Road).
